us-politics.headline: Trump Warns Iran After Airman Rescue, Rejects Ceasefire Offer
us-politics.body:
Trump held a press conference Sunday touting the rescue of a wounded US airman shot down over Iran, calling it a historic mission and warning that Iran could be 'taken out in one night.' He revealed Iran had submitted a ceasefire proposal, calling it 'significant' but 'not good enough.' Negotiations remain deadlocked after Iran publicly rejected the US peace proposal as 'unrealistic.'

Israel struck an Iranian petrochemical plant ahead of Trump's deadline, raising pressure on Tehran. One House Republican predicted US troop deployment, saying he sees no other path. JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon warned a prolonged conflict could tip the economy into recession. Senator Bernie Sanders called Trump's profanity-laced Iran threat the 'ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual.' A top GOP defense voice argued the US could reopen the Strait of Hormuz without a ground invasion.

sources.title: Live updates: Trump details officer’s ‘historic’ rescue, says all of Iran ‘could be taken out in one night’
Trump touts airman rescue mission, boasts Iran could be 'taken out in 1 night'
Trump: Iran ceasefire proposal ‘significant,’ ‘not good enough’
Top GOP defense voice breaks with invasion fears, says US can reopen Hormuz without ground war
Trump reveals Iran made 'significant proposal' after ultimatum, but 'not good enough'
Israel hits petrochemical plant in Iran ahead of Trump deadline
Dimon warns Iran war could sink economy into a recession
Iran spurns ‘unrealistic’ US peace proposal
Sanders on Trump Iran f-bomb threat: ‘Ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual’
House Republican predicts Trump will deploy troops in Iran: ‘I just don’t see any other way’

us-politics.headline: Supreme Court Backs Bannon; DOJ Flynn Settlement Faces Scrutiny
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court sided with Steve Bannon in his bid to dismiss his January 6 contempt conviction, a significant legal win for the former Trump strategist. The ruling adds to a series of legal battles reshaping accountability for the Capitol riot.

Separately, Representative Jamie Raskin demanded the Justice Department hand over details of a settlement reached with Michael Flynn, raising questions about whether the DOJ is offering favorable treatment to Trump allies. Trump also vowed to pursue whoever leaked information about the downed pilot's rescue mission in Iran, framing it as a national security matter. Critics see the threat as an attempt to suppress press scrutiny of military operations.

sources.title: Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in bid to dismiss Jan. 6 conviction
Raskin demands DOJ turn over details on settlement with Michael Flynn
Trump says US will go after ‘leaker’ of info on downed pilot in Iran

us-politics.headline: Trump Accounts Launch, AI Blueprint, and Tesla Probe Closed
us-politics.body:
BNY Mellon will manage the new 'Trump Accounts' program ahead of its official launch, with the Treasury Department overseeing the initiative. Details on eligibility and structure remain limited.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman released a policy blueprint calling for federal regulation and taxation of artificial intelligence, a notable move from a leading industry figure. Meanwhile, federal regulators closed their investigation into Tesla's 'Actually Smart Summon' feature, clearing the autonomous driving tool without enforcement action. The closure follows years of scrutiny over the safety of Tesla's driver-assistance systems.

sources.title: BNY to manage Trump Accounts ahead of program’s launch
OpenAI’s Altman releases blueprint for taxing, regulating artificial intelligence
Regulator closes investigation into Tesla ‘actually smart summon’ feature

us-politics.headline: Democrats Struggle to Rebuild as Internal Divisions Persist
us-politics.body:
Opinion writers and analysts are questioning whether Democrats can capitalize on Trump's controversies, arguing the party faces structural and messaging problems that blunt any political advantage. One Hill analysis warns Democrats have too many internal troubles to simply wait for Trump to fail.

A separate piece challenges the Christian nationalist movement's claim to represent mainstream Christianity, arguing the coalition is fragile and theologically contested. The Republican Party also faces its own fault lines, with Representative Don Bacon publicly criticizing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's firing of top military officers as 'not morally right nor wise.' Dissent from within GOP ranks on both military and foreign policy signals growing unease ahead of midterm positioning.

sources.title: Democrats have too many troubles to cheer Trump’s failures
Christian nationalism versus actual Christianity
Don Bacon on Hegseth firing top military officers: ‘Not morally right nor wise’

au-politics.headline: Iran War Escalates as Ceasefire Rejected, AI Targeting Revealed
au-politics.body:
Iran has rejected a US proposal for a 45-day ceasefire, keeping the conflict live as Trump held a press conference praising the rescue of a downed American airman. The rejection signals Tehran is not ready to de-escalate on Washington's terms.

Separately, US officials say Chinese AI is helping Iran target American forces with what they describe as "incredible precision," using satellite intelligence to guide strikes. The claim draws Beijing directly into the conflict's technology dimension, raising the stakes well beyond a bilateral US-Iran confrontation.

Together, the developments point to a war that is widening in scope and complexity, with no clear off-ramp in sight.

sources.title: Trump praises airman's rescue as Iran rejects 45-day ceasefire proposal
Chinese AI helping Iran target US forces with 'incredible precision'

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Bites: Outback Tourism Collapses, Sydney Turns to Bikes
au-politics.body:
Soaring fuel prices are reshaping how Australians move. In Sydney, commuters are ditching cars for bicycles in growing numbers, citing costs they describe as "crazy." Cycling infrastructure is under pressure as the shift accelerates.

The pain is sharper in remote areas. An outback Queensland pub that normally draws Easter crowds hosted just two visitors this year, with the roadhouse operator linking the collapse directly to fuel costs deterring long-distance travel. Regional tourism operators are absorbing the hit with little buffer.

The Spectator has separately published commentary framing broader cost-of-living pressures as evidence of government failure, though it stops short of proposing specific policy alternatives. The fuel squeeze is cutting across urban and rural Australia in different but compounding ways.

sources.title: Cars make way for bikes as Sydney commuters saddle up to circumvent ‘crazy’ fuel costs
Outback tourist pub had two visitors this Easter amid fuel price pinch
Free online therapy from a headache-inducing government

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila Threatens Queensland Weeks After Narelle's Destruction
au-politics.body:
A second severe tropical cyclone is bearing down on far north Queensland, just three weeks after Cyclone Narelle caused significant damage to the same region. Maila is tracking toward the coast and has been classified as severe, raising fears communities have had insufficient time to recover.

Back-to-back cyclones in a single season stretch emergency services, insurance systems, and local infrastructure. The timing is particularly difficult for small coastal and agricultural communities still clearing debris from Narelle.

Authorities are urging residents to prepare now. The pattern raises broader questions about the frequency of intense cyclone events in the region.

sources.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila could hit far north Queensland three weeks after Narelle tore through

au-politics.headline: Scam Empire Alleged in Timor-Leste; AI Risks in Environmental Reviews
au-politics.body:
An investigation has exposed alleged links between a sanctioned criminal scam network known as the Prince Group and undeveloped resort land in Timor-Leste, with private jets and deserted shorelines central to the story. The findings suggest the group used the young nation's regulatory gaps to embed financial interests.

On a separate governance front, scientists are warning that using AI to prepare and assess environmental impact statements risks repeating the failures of the robodebt scheme - automated decisions made without adequate human oversight, accountability, or error-correction. The concern is that governments may adopt AI tools to speed up approvals without understanding their limits.

Both stories point to the same underlying problem: oversight mechanisms failing to keep pace with new and complex risks.

sources.title: Private jets, deserted shores and an unbuilt resort: alleged links to sanctioned ‘scam’ empire revealed in Timor-Leste
Using AI to prepare and evaluate environmental assessments risks ‘robodebt-style’ failures, scientists say

world.headline: Trump's Iran Deadline Looms as Ceasefire Talks Intensify
world.body:
President Trump has declared his deadline for Iran "final" while simultaneously threatening to destroy Iranian power plants and bridges - warnings that Democrats have condemned as potential war crimes. Republicans have largely backed the posture. Iran is pushing its own ceasefire proposal, and Pakistan has offered a two-phased truce deal, though Iran has refused to reopen the Strait of Hormuz under any temporary arrangement.

A successful US operation to rescue an American airman from inside Iran has added a volatile dimension to negotiations. Trump publicly praised the rescue at a White House Easter event. Analysts warn Washington may also attempt to exploit ethnic divisions within Iran as additional leverage. The clock is running.

sources.title: Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive
Trump warns deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war
Trump praised airman’s rescue in Iran during White House Easter event
Where are Iran’s power plants that Trump has threatened to destroy?
In maps and photos: Five Iranian civilian bridges at risk of US strikes
Bombing of Iran's pharma producers threatens patients' lives
The US could still try to play the ethnic card in Iran
How US operation to rescue air officer from Iran unfolded
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Has Trump confirmed Iran’s claim that protesters were US-armed?
Pakistan offers ‘two-phased’ truce deal to end US-Israel war on Iran
Trump’s ‘Bridge Day’ threat: Can a last-ditch ceasefire plan work?

world.headline: Global Energy Crisis Deepens as Hormuz and Libya Tensions Bite
world.body:
The ongoing conflict around the Strait of Hormuz has made Asia the epicenter of a worsening oil crisis, with supply disruptions rippling across global markets. In Egypt, Cairo is dimming its lights as energy prices soar. European governments are watching Libya's internal oil disputes with alarm, fearing they mirror the Hormuz blockade and could further squeeze already strained supplies.

Iran has threatened to close the Bab al-Mandeb strait as well, a move that would choke a critical artery for world trade. Ukraine struck the Russian Black Sea energy hub at Novorossiysk, and Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian refineries are now being felt as far as St Petersburg.

sources.title: How Asia became ground zero for the oil crisis
Cairo faces darker nights as global energy prices continue to soar
Libya’s oil disputes mirror Hormuz crisis, fuel European energy fears
Ukraine strikes Russian Black Sea energy hub Novorossiysk
‘Smell’ of war comes to St Petersburg as Ukraine hammers Russian refineries
Iran threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure: How would that affect world trade?

world.headline: Gaza Death Toll Mounts as WHO Vehicle Hit, Lebanon Struck
world.body:
Israeli forces fired on a World Health Organization vehicle in southern Gaza, killing one person, according to medics. The strike is the latest in a pattern of attacks on humanitarian operations in the territory. Young Palestinians face mass unemployment as Israel maintains its grip on Gaza's economy, leaving an entire generation with no viable path forward.

Israel also intensified operations in Lebanon, destroying towns in the south and striking areas around Beirut previously designated as safe zones. The widening front is compounding a humanitarian crisis that already spans multiple countries.

sources.title: Gaza camp offers children a sense of safety
Israeli army fire on WHO vehicle in southern Gaza kills one, medics report
Gaza grandmother loses her family due to Israeli attacks
Jobless young Palestinians trapped as Israel holds Gaza’s economy hostage
Israel destroys southern Lebanon towns, hits ‘safe’ areas around Beirut

world.headline: Myanmar Junta Chief Takes Presidency; Kim's Daughter Eyes Succession
world.body:
Myanmar's military chief Min Aung Hlaing has been formally elected president, consolidating power after the 2021 coup. The move raises questions about whether the junta will use the title to seek international legitimacy or simply entrench military rule further.

In North Korea, South Korean intelligence now assesses that Kim Jong Un's daughter, Kim Ju Ae, is the most likely successor to her father. Her increasingly prominent public appearances have fueled the assessment. Two authoritarian states, two succession questions - both with significant regional consequences.

sources.title: North Korea: Kim's daughter now seen as likely heir — South
Myanmar junta chief elected president — what comes next?

ai.headline: Iran Threatens OpenAI's Stargate Data Center in UAE
ai.body:
Iran has issued threats against OpenAI's Stargate AI data center under construction in Abu Dhabi, raising security concerns about the ambitious $500 billion infrastructure project. The threats mark a notable escalation, targeting physical AI infrastructure rather than digital systems.

The timing is awkward for OpenAI, which has been publicly pitching an optimistic vision of AI's economic future - including public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day work week. Building the physical backbone of that future just got more complicated. Geopolitical risk is now a real variable in data center site selection, and the Gulf region's appeal as an AI hub faces a new stress test.

sources.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day work week

ai.headline: AI Tools Reshape Work, But the Data Remains Murky
ai.body:
MIT Technology Review highlights a persistent problem: the data needed to measure AI's real impact on jobs simply does not exist in useful form. Economists and policymakers are flying blind as automation reshapes labor markets, relying on surveys and proxies rather than hard evidence.

Meanwhile, AI is visibly changing behavior at the edges of the economy. Small online sellers are using tools like Alibaba's Accio to decide what products to make, shifting creative and commercial decisions to algorithms. Separately, a growing GitHub thread documents Claude Code degrading significantly for complex engineering tasks after a February update - a reminder that AI capability can move backwards as well as forwards.

sources.title: The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI
AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
Claude Code is unusable for complex engineering tasks with the Feb updates

ai.headline: Adobe's Hosts File Stunt Highlights App Overreach Backlash
ai.body:
Adobe was caught silently modifying users' hosts files to check whether Creative Cloud is installed - a practice security-conscious users typically associate with malware, not enterprise software. The move drew sharp criticism for being both invasive and unnecessary.

The story landed alongside a broader conversation about app overreach. A widely shared post argued that web versions of products are preferable to forced app downloads, and an 81-year-old Dodgers fan made headlines after being locked out of buying tickets because he lacks a smartphone. The thread connecting all three: companies increasingly treating access to their products as leverage over user behavior.

sources.title: Adobe modifies hosts file to detect whether Creative Cloud is installed
I won't download your app. The web version is a-ok
81yo Dodgers fan can no longer get tickets because he doesn't have a smartphone

ai.headline: Intel's Packaging Bet and the Race to Build AI Infrastructure
ai.body:
Wired profiles Intel's long-shot wager on advanced chip packaging technology as a path back to relevance in the AI boom. While Nvidia dominates on raw compute, Intel is betting that how chips connect to each other - not just what they can do individually - will determine the next phase of AI hardware.

Elsewhere in infrastructure, Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins floated the idea of data centers in space, citing power and cooling constraints on Earth. Spain's Xoople raised $130 million to build AI-ready geospatial mapping data. The picture is consistent: the race to build AI's physical and data foundations is accelerating fast, and the bets are getting larger and stranger.

sources.title: The Ridiculously Nerdy Intel Bet That Could Rake in Billions
Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins wants data centers in space
Spain’s Xoople raises $130 million Series B to map the Earth for AI

btc.headline: Bitcoin Hovers Near $70K as Iran Tensions Drive Volatility
btc.body:
Bitcoin climbed above $70,000 Monday before profit-taking pulled it back, with geopolitical noise doing most of the driving. Reports of Iran ceasefire talks sparked a brief rally, while Trump's hardening ultimatum toward Tehran kept traders on edge. Prediction markets are now functioning as real-time macro sensors, with Iran war contracts on Polymarket and Kalshi tracking sentiment faster than traditional news cycles.

Contrarian indicators suggest a potential bottom is forming, but analysts warn a clean break above $70,000 requires sustained buying pressure. Strategy added another 4,871 BTC for $330 million, pushing its holdings near 767,000 BTC despite paper losses exceeding $14.5 billion in Q1. The accumulation signals institutional conviction even as retail traders stay cautious.

sources.title: Can Trump’s ultimatum to Iran push Bitcoin price back up to $75K?
Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge
Bitcoin profit taking keeps BTC below $70K as Trump doubles down on Iran
Strategy adds $330M BTC as paper losses top $14.5B in Q1
Strategy added another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, with holdings nearing 767,000 BTC
Bitcoin rallies on report of Iran ceasefire talks, Algorand extends gains
First real bull signal since 2025? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
Iran war bets turn prediction markets into real-time macro radar: Sygnum

btc.headline: US Crypto Legislation Advances as Tokenization Divides Institutions
btc.body:
A Senate Banking panel member confirmed an April timeline for crypto market structure legislation, adding momentum to what has been a slow-moving regulatory process. The news arrives as tokenization splits opinion at the highest levels of finance. Jamie Dimon told JPMorgan staff the bank must move faster to keep pace with tokenized finance, while the IMF warned that tokenization risks importing crypto volatility directly into global markets.

Bernstein analysts argued Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could double its stock price, reflecting growing private-sector conviction. China's tax authority separately urged domestic banks to adopt blockchain for lending services - a notable signal from a government that has otherwise restricted crypto activity.

sources.title: US Senate Banking panel member confirms April timeline for crypto market structure
Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could help double stock price: Bernstein
Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance
China's tax authority urges banks to implement blockchain for lending services
IMF warns tokenization could bring crypto risks into global financial markets

btc.headline: Polymarket Upgrades Exchange; Kalshi Wins Court Battle in New Jersey
btc.body:
Prediction markets had a consequential weekend. Polymarket announced a full exchange overhaul, moving to control its own order book and resolution infrastructure rather than relying on third-party systems. The upgrade is a direct bid for credibility as these platforms attract serious macro traders.

Kalshi won a federal appeals court ruling blocking New Jersey from shutting down its sports prediction markets, a significant legal victory that strengthens the case for prediction markets operating outside traditional gambling frameworks. Together the two developments mark a maturation moment for an industry that spent years fighting for legitimacy.

sources.title: Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
Appeals court blocks New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports markets
Iran war bets turn prediction markets into real-time macro radar: Sygnum

btc.headline: Aave Loses Risk Manager as DeFi Governance Tensions Deepen
btc.body:
Chaos Labs, one of Aave's primary risk managers, is departing the protocol amid a broader contributor exodus and unresolved governance disputes. The exit raises questions about how decentralized protocols retain specialized talent when disagreements over direction and compensation spill into the open.

Separately, onchain perpetual DEX volumes have fallen for five consecutive months since an October peak, with Hyperliquid absorbing an outsized share of remaining activity. The combination of governance friction at a top DeFi protocol and shrinking derivatives volume points to a sector still working through a post-peak hangover.

sources.title: Aave loses key risk manager, Chaos Labs, amid contributor exodus and disputes
Onchain perp DEX volumes fall for five straight months after October peak

food.name: Miso-Glazed Aubergine (Nasu Dengaku)
food.description: Halved aubergines broiled until caramelised under a sticky white miso, mirin, and sake glaze
food.tip: Score the flesh in a crosshatch before glazing so the miso penetrates deeply rather than just sitting on the surface

exercise.name: Monday Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or dumbbells, a clear corridor or outdoor path
exercise.description: Cycle through farmer carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries at varying weights to build grip strength, core stability, and real-world functional fitness in a way most gym routines completely neglect

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — and when applied to humans, whose cells, beliefs, and memories constantly change, it becomes a sharp challenge to the idea of a continuous self.
learn.why: On a Monday at the start of a new week it is a quietly destabilising and productive question to sit with: if you are not the same person you were five years ago, who exactly made last week's commitments — and who is responsible for keeping them?
